author: Luigi Fontana
chapter: Chapter 9: The Modern Healthy Longevity Diet

claims.text: Walnut consumption is associated with lower all-cause mortality in long-term prospective studies.
claims.mechanism: Walnuts are rich in alpha-linolenic acid (ALA), polyphenols, and L-arginine which reduce systemic inflammation, improve endothelial function, and modulate the gut microbiome.
claims.recommendation: 30g (about 7 whole walnuts) daily, ideally as a snack or sprinkled on salads
claims.reference: Bao et al. NEJM 2013 — Harvard prospective study, n=118,962 over 30 years

consumption.preparation: raw, unsalted; never roasted at high heat (oxidizes ALA)
consumption.reasoning: High heat destroys the heat-sensitive omega-3 fats that drive most of the cardiovascular benefit

relationships.note: ALA from walnuts and MUFA from olive oil together produce a stronger lipid-profile improvement than either alone (PREDIMED substudy).
